info.title: ITFlow API
info.description: API for ITFlow, an open-source IT documentation and management tool for MSPs. Provides access to clients, contacts, assets, tickets, invoices, and other IT management resources.

get.description: Retrieve assets. Returns all assets for the client scope of the API key, or filter by specific parameters.

asset_id.description: ID of the asset to update

post.description: Delete an existing IT asset and its associated interfaces.

asset_id.description: ID of the asset to delete

post.description: Create a new client. Requires an API key with 'All Clients' scope.

client_id.description: ID of the client to update

post.description: Archive a client and disable their recurring invoices.

client_id.description: ID of the client to archive
ID of the client to unarchive

parameters.name: contact_phone_or_mobile
parameters.description: Filter by phone or mobile number

post.description: Create a new contact for a client.

contact_id.description: ID of the contact to update
ID of the contact to delete

post.description: Archive a contact and their associated user account.

contact_id.description: ID of the contact to archive

post.description: Restore a previously archived contact and their associated user account.

contact_id.description: ID of the contact to unarchive

get.description: Retrieve credential records. Requires the API key decrypt password.

parameters.name: api_key_decrypt_password
parameters.description: Decryption password for the API key

credential_id.description: ID of the credential to update

api_key_decrypt_password.description: Decryption password for the API key

post.description: Update an existing document. A version history entry is created automatically.

document_id.description: ID of the document to update

get.description: Retrieve expense records. Filtering by expense_id requires an API key with 'All Clients' scope.

parameters.name: expense_id
parameters.description: Filter by expense ID (requires all-client scope)

post.description: Mark a support ticket as resolved.

ticket_id.description: ID of the ticket to resolve

apiKeyQuery.name: api_key
apiKeyQuery.description: API key passed as a query parameter for GET requests. For POST requests, include the api_key in the JSON request body.

offsetParam.name: offset
offsetParam.description: Number of records to skip for pagination

Unauthorized.description: Authentication failed - invalid or missing API key

count.description: Number of rows affected or returned

data.description: The requested, created, or modified data

client_id.description: Client ID (required when API key has all-client scope)

asset_type.description: Type of asset (e.g. workstation, server, printer)

lead.description: Whether this is a lead (1) or client (0)

primary.description: Whether this is the primary contact

important.description: Whether this contact is marked as important

billing.description: Whether this is a billing contact

technical.description: Whether this is a technical contact

important.description: Whether this credential is marked as important

api_key_decrypt_password.description: Decryption password for the API key

folder.description: Folder ID to place the document in

primary.description: Whether this is the primary location

priority.description: Priority level (e.g. Low, Medium, High, Critical)

contact.description: Contact ID associated with the ticket

asset.description: Asset ID associated with the ticket

assigned_to.description: Technician user ID to assign the ticket to
